transcript.text: Good, thanks.
How did the QBR session go yesterday?
Well, pretty good.
Yeah, we, like, we've, like, recovered a lot of the traffic we lost in Q2.
Nice.
Hey, Matt.
Victoria.
Hello.
Okay, I think Mara might join a bit late, so we can get started real quick.
Let me share my screen.
Okay, so just going through the updates for each category.
So we've upgraded our Looker reporting a little bit, so you can see some fancy new colors over here.
I'll just run through the Looker report itself in a bit, but just going through the different categories, we saw a jump for the blog page, for the blog pages, the cash flow.
Hedge versus Fair Value, that particular block saw the most jump, and calculating late fees on invoices as well.
For the Per Diem pages, we were continuing to lose a few sessions over the past couple of weeks now.
Texas, Florida, Virginia lost the most.
Generally, no change in average positions, but still we're losing a few clicks.
So far, we think it's seasonal.
For the expense category pages, for a change, we had a gain, reversing the trend over the past few weeks.
Pretty much every page saw a gain in overall sessions.
Stamp duty, security system, pest control, and signage were the most prolific pages.
And generally, we saw higher CTRs in this cluster last week.
So yeah, good news over here.
For the tuning pages, pretty much maintain performance at the usual sort of range.

What I'm used to, assets liabilities and audit trails, which have been traditionally the best performers over here, continued their performance for the second batch of tuning pages, budget variance and quarterly taxes gained a few clicks, but generally it was a low week compared to a couple of weeks ago for the treasury pages.
Again, a pretty small batch of pages, but you have the liquidity position report, which unexpectedly has been doing quite well, gained a lot of clicks, so we saw good performance there.
For the vendor pages, overall, we saw a higher level of sessions.
Generally, again, and I'll walk through the Looker just shortly, but generally, you know, in the Looker, we've broken down vendor pages by the type of page as well as the category.
So, the versus pages, you know, as we can see from this chart over here, is driving the most traffic.
The AI cluster is also the highest in terms of traffic, followed by content creation, and project management for the past month has also been doing quite well.
Just hopping over to the Looker, the link is at the top of the agenda over here, so you can just click that at any time.
So yeah, we just have the usual tabs over here.
We're pulling in data from GA4 and GSC.
We weren't sure which of these would be highly relevant to you, so if you do have some requests for additional metrics or additional deep ties, please let us know.
The tab over here, Vendor Cohorts, this is where really we're pulling in data on the vendor pages itself.
So I've just filtered this for the past month.
So as we can see, generally, the versus pages are driving the most traffic, followed by the overview.
Alternatives, as we've observed so far, they don't seem to be attracting a
A of attention in general.

In terms of the sort of page categories themselves, we have quite a few, but generally AI has been the best performer in terms of overall clicks, followed by content creation, project management, DevOps as well, has been doing pretty recently.
We're seeing pretty high engagement rates over here.
I was wondering about that.
I was wondering if in your GA4, maybe if the threshold is set a little too low, perhaps.
Not sure if, you know, it's not a major reporting metric, I think for these pages, but just wanted to flag that, like we're seeing 98% engagement rates, which is a bit of an anomaly.
So just something we wanted to check.
But yeah, generally, you know, please do let us know if you wanna see something else in terms of reporting, you'd be happy to build that out.
So yeah, I'm gonna stop monologuing.
Any questions?
I can answer about any of this at all.
Are you measuring engagement rate?
We're just pulling this directly from GA4.
So I think, yeah, we're not applying any filters to it.
Yeah.
So usually it's set at a minute, I believe that's the threshold, but just looking at these numbers, I'm wondering if, yeah, like DevOps is like 99%, which is over a hundred sessions.
So it's quite a lot.
Could you scroll up a bit back to, this is really helpful, by the way.
Thank you for putting it together.
I'm just curious, trying to see if the versus pages, I mean, we have a lot more versus pages than the others.
If this is the total or if it's like an average per page.
But this is.
The total.
So we can build out an average as well, average sessions.
It's fairly easy enough, but yeah.
Got it.
Okay.
So yeah, that was the performance update in general.
Just hopping over the content refreshes.
We don't have many URLs in general here, but the procurement articles are generally doing quite well in this small cluster.
We are delivering two more pages tomorrow.
So yeah, waiting for your feedback and edits on that.
Just want to give an update.
We have automated the overview page publishing for the vendor pages.
There's just one part which is not automated yet, which is the image generation.
So generating the two images for each.
vendor.
We do have that portion of the request in progress.
So that part is still manual, but generally we can publish pages into your CMS automatically.
We use a few test vendors.
So with the next patch, we'd like to get started with like live pages.
The process stages the page.
It does not directly publish it.
So we're still doing a manual review just to make sure that, you know, the QA is good.
But yeah, next step there is, you know, whenever we get started with the next patch, we'll test it out in production.
And then we're also building out the automation for the alternatives and versus pages.
So pretty, pretty good news over there.
So yeah.
And yeah, following up on that to let us know about the next patch of vendor pages, we just wanted to check in terms of, you know, your plans for the timeline, etc.
Just wanted to check up regarding that.

Yeah, so I'll gather the next vendors, but I also wanted to move, like, at the same time, I wanted to make progress on using the existing data we have there for these vendors and going more granular, as we were talking about, like, with the segments or industries.
So do you think you could, maybe we can discuss next week, like, on next week's call, like, an initial plan on how you think would be the best way for us to do that?
Yeah, sure, not a problem.
We can, we'll put that together, yeah.
That'd be great, thank you.
And, yeah, I didn't notice over here, Matt, but RefreshPage is coming your way, two of those tomorrow, and we're...
I'm looking forward to your feedback on the existing ones.
So, yeah, that's pretty much it for the update.
So, yeah, if there's any other questions, we can answer.
What images need to be generated?
The review is basically the vendor image.
We have two images.
It's the image of the logo.
So there's a vendor logo, and there's a vendor logo and the name.
So it's, like, two images.
And it's a bit finicky because I think the background needs to be transparent.
It needs to be a certain size.
So that's the only part that's manual.
I wonder if the alternatives are not doing very well because on the primary review page, we've got an alternative section, and then we've got another, like, we've got alternatives each.
We've
And then we've got an alternatives, another H2, like duplicate H2s and like the written part is on the main page.
And then like the link itself, the alternatives page is competitors.
You know what I mean, Victoria?
Which page are you looking at?
I'm just on active campaign.
I could, I could share real quick.
Yes.
I think we could, we could make these changes pretty quickly.
So I'm just on the primary active campaign.
Like, so we've got this alternative section, which I bet is getting crawled quite a lot because it's, you know, that's what LLMs are after.
And then we've got this alternative.
It's H2.
And then the link to the alternatives page is competitors.
Yeah, we, we talked about this, um, like not including that, that section, um, like the one that we actually, that we list the, the competitors only leaving the one that's already hard coded.
So I think we should, as we move forward, we should do that and maybe deleting that, that section from the, the pages that are already live.
I think we did that for, uh, a few batches on the beginning.
Yeah, I, I think, um, yeah, I remember Nika doing it for one of the earlier batches.
I'll check, um, why it wasn't deleted for the active campaign and we'll check the other pages as well, just to make sure that the, um, the hard coded lists are not in there.
Yeah, yeah, but good catch, Luke.
Okay.
Yeah, we have to make fixes in the template.
You can also do that.
I'm looking at a few other ones, the 1Password, and they don't have that section.
So, yeah, if you can go through them and then just see which ones we still have that alternatives section and delete from this, that would be great.
Yep, we will do that.
I think the latest batches don't have it.
It's some of the batches in between that I think we missed that.
So we will take a look at it and we'll fix it.
Cool.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Thanks.
That's all I heard.
That was pretty much it.
Mara, was there anything else?
No.
Sorry to join late again, you guys.
My call right before this tends to run over.
And anyway, thank you for starting and nothing else to add.
Cool.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
Thank you.
Good to see all of you.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly check-in between Ramp and GrowthX to discuss content performance, updates, and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Blog traffic recovered from Q2 losses; cash flow and late fees content performed well](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=10.0)
  - [Vendor pages saw increased sessions, with "versus" pages and AI cluster driving most traffic](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [Automated publishing for vendor overview pages implemented; alternatives and versus page automation in progress](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
  - [Plan needed for leveraging existing vendor data for more granular content (segments/industries)](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Updates

  - [Blog: Cash flow hedge vs. fair value and late fees on invoices saw significant jumps](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)
  - [Per Diem: Slight session loss (Texas, Florida, Virginia most affected); likely seasonal](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)
  - [Expense category: Gained sessions, reversing recent trend; stamp duty, security system, pest control, and signage pages performed well](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=86.0)
  - [Tuning pages: Stable performance; assets, liabilities, and audit trails continued strong](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=113.0)
  - [Treasury: Liquidity position report unexpectedly gained clicks](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Vendor pages: Higher overall sessions; "versus" pages driving most traffic](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
      - [AI cluster highest traffic, followed by content creation and project management](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)

### Looker Reporting Upgrades

  - [New color scheme implemented](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=41.0)
  - [Vendor Cohorts tab added: Shows versus pages driving most traffic, followed by overview pages](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=217.0)
  - [Alternatives pages underperforming](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=235.0)
  - [Engagement rates suspiciously high (98-99%); potential GA4 threshold issue to investigate](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)

### Content Refresh and Automation

  - [Procurement articles performing well in small cluster](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=387.0)
  - [Two more pages to be delivered tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=396.0)
  - [Vendor overview page publishing automated, except for image generation](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
  - [Next steps: Test automation with live pages, build out alternatives and versus page automation](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)

### Vendor Page Strategy

  - [Plan needed for leveraging existing vendor data for more granular content (segments/industries)](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [GrowthX to present initial plan in next week's call](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)

### Alternatives Page Performance Issue

  - [Potential duplicate content problem identified (alternatives section on main page + separate alternatives page)](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
  - [Action: Remove hardcoded alternatives list from vendor pages, update template](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to prepare plan for leveraging existing vendor data (segments/industries)](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [Review and provide feedback on upcoming refresh pages](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [Test automated publishing with next batch of live vendor pages](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=438.0)
  - [GrowthX to audit vendor pages for duplicate alternatives sections and remove as needed](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=709.0)
  - [Investigate high engagement rate anomaly in GA4 reporting](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)
  - [Victoria to gather next set of vendor pages for content creation](https://fathom.video/share/TsZsBFxM3gn8a3PsPxxehZbWydTcKy9b?tab=summary&timestamp=465.0)

action_items.description: Gather next batch of vendor pages for automation testing
Prepare initial plan for using existing vendor data to create more granular content (segments/industries). Present next week.
Review and provide feedback on 2 new RefreshPages being delivered tomorrow
Review and provide feedback on 2 new RefreshPages being delivered tomorrow
Audit all vendor pages. Remove duplicate alternatives section where present. Update template if needed.
